title: A Quiet Place: Day One Opening Weekend Box Office Results
text: A Quiet Place: Day One achieved strong box office performance in its debut weekend. The film earned $22.5 million on its opening day, including $6.8 million from Thursday previews, both new franchise records. Forecasts for the full opening weekend ranged from $53 million to potentially $55 million, which would mark the best opening in the franchise's history, surpassing the original film's $50.2 million debut in 2018 and Part II's $47.5 million opening in 2021. The prequel, directed by Michael Sarnoski with story input from franchise creator John Krasinski, is set in New York City during the initial alien invasion and features mostly new characters, with Djimon Hounsou reprising his role from the previous installment. The film's success comes as horror movies have struggled at the box office in 2024, making this performance particularly noteworthy for the genre.
